title: Halt Condition Schema
description: Schema for halt conditions in the SocioTechnical Constitution runtime. A halt condition defines when the system must stop and require human or governance intervention.

id.description: Unique halt condition identifier. Convention: HALT_ followed by all-caps snake_case.

trigger.description: Human-readable description of what causes this halt condition to activate.

trigger_expression.description: Optional machine-evaluable expression or rule ID that maps to this halt. Used by the Policy Engine.

resolution.description: Prescribed resolution path: what must happen before the halt is cleared.

status.description: Current state of this halt condition instance.

severity.description: Effect level. warn = flag only; block = prevent action; critical = page Council.

article_ref.description: Reference to the Article in SOCIOTECHNICAL_CONSTITUTION.md that this halt enforces. E.g. Article VIII, Invariant 3.

component.description: Which runtime component owns this halt condition evaluation.

activated_at.description: When this halt condition was last activated. Null if never activated.

resolved_at.description: When this halt condition was last resolved. Null if currently active or never triggered.

resolved_by.description: User or role who cleared the halt condition.

override_id.description: If status is overridden, the ID of the break-glass override record.

evidence_ref.description: Path to the evidence log entry capturing this halt event.

auto_resolve_on.description: Condition string that, if true, allows automatic resolution without manual action.
